title: Support consecutive repetition [*N:M], [+], and [*] in sequence expressions
body:
## Summary

This patch extends Verilator's consecutive repetition support to cover range
and unbounded forms -- `[*N:M]`, `[+]` (= `[*1:$]`), and `[*]` (= `[*0:$]`)
-- when they appear as the leading element of a sequence expression (e.g.,
`a[*1:3] ##1 b`). Standalone exact `[*N]` (N >= 2) was already handled by
`V3AssertPre`; that path is unchanged.

## Reproducer

```systemverilog
module t (input clk);
  logic a, b;
  assert property (@(posedge clk) a[*1:3] ##1 b);  // range
  assert property (@(posedge clk) a[+]    ##1 b);  // one-or-more
  assert property (@(posedge clk) a[*]    ##1 b);  // zero-or-more
endmodule
```
On master:

```
%Error-UNSUPPORTED: t.sv:3: Unsupported: consecutive repetition range in sequence expression
%Error-UNSUPPORTED: t.sv:4: Unsupported: consecutive repetition range in sequence expression
%Error-UNSUPPORTED: t.sv:5: Unsupported: consecutive repetition range in sequence expression
```
## Changes

- src/V3AstNodeExpr.h: extend AstConsRep with op3 maxCountp (Optional[AstNodeExpr]) and m_unbounded bool; add second constructor for range/unbounded forms; update sameNode, dump, dumpJson.
- src/V3AstNodes.cpp: implement AstConsRep::dump and dumpJson.
- src/verilog.y: add grammar rules for [*N:M], [+], [*] in sexpr context; remove stale BBUNSUP placeholders for these forms from boolean_abbrev.
- src/V3Width.cpp: validate maxCountp -- must be constant, >= min, and non-zero (IEEE 1800-2023 16.9.2).
- src/V3AssertPre.cpp: add early return for range/unbounded forms so they reach V3AssertProp instead of the saturating-counter path.
- src/V3AssertProp.cpp: add AssertPropConsRepVisitor -- runs before DFA graph building; intercepts AstSExpr nodes whose leading operand is AstConsRep; lowers to a PExpr forward-looking loop (count cycles of expr up to min, then try successor). Special-cases minN == 0 to skip an always-true cnt >= 0 comparison that would trigger -UNSIGNED. Standalone [+] and [*] lower to the expression itself (shortest non-vacuous match, matching simulator behavior). Standalone range with minN >= 2 reports UNSUPPORTED. Trailing ConsRep in a sequence expression reports UNSUPPORTED.

## Tests

- t_assert_prop_consec_rep: CRC functional test extended with` [*1:3] ##1 b, [+] ##1 b`, standalone` [+] |-> b, and [*] |-> b` cases; expected failure counts calibrated against simulation.
- t_assert_prop_consec_rep_bad: error cases -- non-constant count, ` max < min `, non-constant max, `[*0], [*N:0]`.
- t_assert_prop_consec_rep_unsup: unsupported cases -- non-`##1` delay, standalone range (`[*2:3] |-> 1`), trailing position (`b ##1 a[+]`).

## Known Limitations

- Trailing position (`b ##1 a[+]`) requires NFA; reports UNSUPPORTED.
- Non-`##1` inter-repetition delay reports UNSUPPORTED.
- Standalone range with minN >= 2 reports UNSUPPORTED.
- `[*N:$] `(half-unbounded, N >= 2) not yet parsed; planned follow-up.

---
Developed by PlanV GmbH, assisted with Claude Code.

Reviewed by YilouWang.
